description: SQL injection — User.where with string interpolation of params[:user][:id]
Broken access control — admin actions exposed without role check (skip_before_action authenticated on new/create)
Command injection — system() with unescaped user-supplied filename interpolated into shell
Stored XSS — current_user.first_name rendered with .html_safe bypassing Rails auto-escaping
Weak password reset — token = Digest::MD5.hexdigest(email) is trivially guessable
Hardcoded Rails secret_key_base in version-controlled file
Plain MD5 password storage — Digest::MD5.hexdigest used for both write and authenticate paths
Excessive data exposure — API returns full user record including password digest in JSON
IDOR — User.find_by(id: params[:user_id]) with no ownership check before update
Vulnerable dependency — outdated gems with known CVEs (Rails / nokogiri / etc.)
